name: Rule 30
description: 1D binary CA with nearest-neighbor rule; from simple seeds it generates highly irregular, chaotic triangular patterns and has been used as a pseudorandom generator.
visual_notes: Good for noisy, flame-like wedges, diagonal streaks, and glitchy triangle fields in 1D→2D space–time plots.

name: Rule 90
description: 1D binary CA that produces Sierpiński-triangle-like fractal structure from a single live cell.
visual_notes: Great for crisp, self-similar triangle lattices; works well as a mask, dithering pattern, or overlay.

name: Rule 110
description: Class IV 1D CA with coexisting regular background and moving localized structures; known to be computationally universal.
visual_notes: Use when you want interacting particles over a textured background; good for glitchy circuitry and signal-flow aesthetics.

name: Rule 184
description: Elementary CA that behaves like a simple one-lane traffic model, conserving the number of particles and forming moving jams and free-flow regions.
visual_notes: Nice for conveyor-belt, scanline-migration, or traffic-flow metaphors and for animating directional drift.

name: Conway's Game of Life
description: Classic Life rule: dead cells with 3 live neighbors are born; live cells with 2 or 3 neighbors survive. Supports gliders, oscillators, and universal computation.
visual_notes: Use for organic pixel swarms, city-like patterns, or growth/decay metaphors. Good base for emergent micro-narratives.

name: HighLife
description: Life variant with an additional birth condition at 6 neighbors; famous for its self-replicating patterns and faster expansion.
visual_notes: Good when you want rapid colonization, self-copying motifs, and aggressively expanding structures.

name: Seeds
description: Every live cell dies each step; dead cells with exactly 2 neighbors are born, producing constantly exploding fronts.
visual_notes: Ideal for sparkling, firework-like, or foam-like growth. Good as a destructive overlay or burst texture.

name: Maze
description: Life-like rule that tends to form static labyrinths and maze-like structures.
visual_notes: Perfect for circuit-board, dungeon, or labyrinth textures and overlays.

name: Day & Night
description: Symmetric Life-like rule where live and dead cells follow complementary rules; supports large organized domains and structures.
visual_notes: Good for high-contrast tilings, domain walls, and patterns that feel reversible (day/night duality).
